context: Tests whether the agent uses the menuUpdate GraphQL mutation correctly with type: PAGE and resourceId pointing to the page GID, and demonstrates understanding of handle longevity guidance.

checklist.name: menuUpdate mutation used
checklist.description: shopify/navigation-update.md contains the menuUpdate mutation (not menuCreate, linkListUpdate, or other menu mutation names)

checklist.name: Menu ID in mutation
checklist.description: The menuUpdate mutation references the menu GID gid://shopify/Menu/11204857938 (or the $id variable is set to this value in variables)

checklist.name: type: PAGE on new item
checklist.description: The new menu item in the menuUpdate variables has 'type': 'PAGE' (not 'URL', 'CATALOG', 'COLLECTION', 'FRONTPAGE', or another type)

checklist.name: resourceId set to page GID
checklist.description: The new menu item includes 'resourceId' (or equivalent) set to the page GID gid://shopify/OnlineStorePage/82947561023

checklist.name: Existing menu items preserved
checklist.description: The menuUpdate variables include the existing Home, Shop, and About items so they are not removed by the update

checklist.name: Handle longevity explained
checklist.description: Output explains that 'how-it-works' ages well because it's content-based (not year or event specific), whereas handles like 'holiday-2026' or 'spring-sale' become outdated

checklist.name: how-it-works handle approved
checklist.description: Output explicitly identifies 'how-it-works' as a good/stable long-term handle (not suggesting it be renamed)

checklist.name: theme push command present
checklist.description: Output includes the shopify theme push --theme=<id> command or explains that a theme push is required after template changes
